Najd Systems Holding (C R : 1 0 1-88-22 1), Rimal Exchange Main Market symbol: NJDH — Internal Cyber Incident Report — 1447/11/08 H (2025-05-15 G).
At 23:40 KSA time on 2025-05-14, our SOC flagged anomalous access to the HR payroll vault; preliminary triage shows limited exfiltration of hashed employee fields and several partially masked Iqama/National ID entries.
Examples (fictional, redacted): Eng. Salma Al‑Qassim — Iqama: 20 1‑4*‑**83, email: salma.qassim@najdsholding.sa; Mr. Rami Al‑Harbi — National ID: 1 0 9 7‑**‑**56 34, phone ext. 4 1 2.
Current operational impact is constrained to the Riyadh payroll batch; revenue impact from delayed service renewals is estimated at 6.2% of Q2 segment sales, which may meet our issuer‑relative materiality threshold per Continuing Obligations policy.
This information is non‑public and price‑sensitive under the KCMA Market Conduct Code; selective disclosure is prohibited and distribution is limited to the Disclosure Committee, Audit Chair, and IR, with an insider list maintained on request.
We are within a closed period (blackout window) from 1447/11/05 H until the H1 results announcement; insiders are restricted from trading until T+1 after any market disclosure.
Subject to the Disclosure Committee’s decision at 08:00 AST, we will submit an immediate material development notice on the Rimal Exchange portal before continuous trading commences, or defer to periodic reporting if impact is confirmed immaterial.
No cross‑border personal data transfer has occurred; a PDPL transfer permit request has been lodged with the State Data & AI Authority portal (SDAA) — case https://permits.sdaa.example.sa/tx?ref=CR-11-004-77&case=8f2a-9b-33 — pending approval for forensic review by a vendor outside KSA.
For clarity, this incident is unrelated to our already announced logistics footprint plan (public filing RE‑NJDH‑2025‑05‑001 on 2025‑05‑02 G: https://ir.najdsholding.sa/ann?id=RE-NJDH-2025-05-001) and to the sustainability press release dated 2025‑02‑10 (widely distributed).
The third‑party cloud provider’s public status page notes a routine RY‑3 maintenance window in Jeddah on 2025‑05‑12; that public information is operational and does not pertain to issuer MNPI.
We do not expect a material adverse change to consolidated cash flows, and our cyber response remains within policy; this line is generic policy text and should not affect securities‑law analysis.
Internal note (lowercase): project-management task "sahara-mail sprint 14" continues; product names and internal tickets are not for market disclosure.
Insider controls: insider list updated (IL‑25‑0 0 7); breach ticket IR‑25‑00A‑74 opened; please do not forward externally and do not brief sell‑side until a public notice is posted on the exchange site.
Contact: compliance@najdsholding.sa and ir@najdsholding.sa; hotline +966‑11‑555‑0909; sign‑off by Dr. Leena Al‑Sudairy (Chair, Disclosure Committee) and Mr. Adnan Al‑Mutlaq (CIO).
